Aerospace Apr 4, 2026

Artemis 2 in good shape cruising towards the moon

A day after lighting its engine to head to the moon, the Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft is performing well with only minor issues.

Artemis 2's successful trans-lunar injection with minimal anomalies validates NASA's deep-space propulsion systems for crewed lunar missions ahead of surface operations.

See SpaceNews →

Regulatory Apr 4, 2026

Trump budget seeks $372 million cut to US Essential Air Service program

US President Donald Trump’s fiscal 2027 budget proposal takes aim at the Essential Air Service program, calling for…...

Eliminating EAS subsidies could force 40+ rural airports to lose commercial service, triggering supply-chain disruptions for regional industries dependent on air connectivity.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Apr 4, 2026

What It’s Like Flying In A Military Transport Aircraft

Uncle Sam's no-frills flying experience.

Military transport operations prioritize cargo and troop capacity over passenger comfort, revealing operational efficiencies that commercial carriers rarely adopt.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Apr 4, 2026

How to Assess Safety Risk

A practical walkthrough of safety risk assessment under 14 CFR Part 5.

Part 5 risk assessment frameworks provide structured methodology for identifying and quantifying safety hazards before incidents occur in regulated aviation operations.

Commercial Apr 3, 2026

What United Airlines' New Relax Row Economy Beds Will Likely Cost On Long-Haul Flights

These beds could come with an appealing price tag.

United's economy bed offering signals carriers are segmenting premium economy to capture mid-tier revenue between standard and business class.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

NASA Sets Coverage for Northrop Grumman’s CRS-24 Resupply Launch

NASA, Northrop Grumman, and SpaceX are targeting no earlier than 8:49 a.m. EDT on Wednesday, April 8, for the next launch delivering science investigations, supplies, and equipment to the International Space Station. Filled with approximately 11,000 pounds of cargo, the Northrop Grumman Cygnus XL...

Northrop Grumman's larger Cygnus XL variant will deliver 11,000 pounds of cargo to the ISS, expanding commercial resupply capacity beyond previous generation capabilities.

See NASA →

Commercial Apr 3, 2026

6 Airlines With The World’s Longest Economy Seat Pitch

Which airlines are currently offering the most seat pitch for their respective economy class products? Here are 6 airlines that have mastered space.

Airlines competing on seat pitch as a cost-effective differentiator reveal how operational efficiency and passenger comfort have become key retention tools in price-competitive markets.

See Simple Flying →

PAI
Safety Apr 4, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to systematically document why residual risks are acceptable after implementing feasible controls, moving beyond theoretical analysis to practical decision-making.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Regulatory Apr 3, 2026

Why Do Airlines Still Use 3-3 Seating On Narrowbody Jets?

Narrowbody aircraft are limited by regulations to max out at 3-3 seating, while changing their fundamental design means they are not narrowbodies.

Airlines cannot exceed 3-3 seating on narrowbodies without redesigning fuselage dimensions, which would reclassify aircraft and trigger costlier certification and operational requirements.

See Simple Flying →

Military Apr 3, 2026

US A-10 hit during Iran rescue mission, pilot ejects and is rescued

A US Air Force A-10 was hit during combat operations tied to the widening war with Iran on…...

Combat damage to active-duty tactical aircraft underscores elevated operational risks in Middle East theaters, affecting maintenance planning and aircraft availability.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Apr 3, 2026

The World’s Largest Air Forces By Annual Budget

A closer look at the world leaders in air power spending.

Military aviation budgets reveal strategic priorities, with spending disparities between superpowers directly reflecting geopolitical competition and regional security concerns.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Apr 4, 2026

FAA Part 5 SMS for Small Operators: Safety Risk Management

This video covers the safety management system (SMS) component safety risk management, which determines the need for, and adequacy of, new or revised risk controls based on the assessment of acceptable risk.

Small operators must now systematically assess and control safety risks rather than relying on reactive incident responses, fundamentally changing how they manage operations.

See NBAA (YouTube) →

Military Apr 3, 2026

US Air Force A-10 Thunderbolt II Crashes Amid Search For Downed F-15 Eagle

The pilot was successfully rescued...

A-10 loss during combat search operations represents continued attrition of legacy airframes in active conflict zones.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 3, 2026

How Boeing Aircraft Have Evolved Since The 747

The introduction of the Boeing 747 in 1969 changed the scale of commercial aviation, but as the industry evolved, so did Boeing's design.

Boeing's post-747 evolution reflects shifting market demands from maximum capacity toward fuel efficiency and operational flexibility, reshaping fleet economics.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Apr 3, 2026

Michael Graham Named NTSB Vice Chairman

The certified transport pilot and NTSB board member will co-head the agency for a three-year term.

Graham's pilot background brings operational expertise to NTSB leadership during a period of increased accident investigation complexity across commercial and general aviation.

See AVweb →

▶20:47
Regulatory Apr 4, 2026

FAA Part 5 SMS for Small Operators: Safety Policy & Safety Promotion

This video provides an overview of implementing a safety management system (SMS), specifically aligned with FAA Part 5 regulations. Join us as we explore Blue Skies Charter, a fictional company, and learn about general SMS requirements, safety policy development and safety promotion strategies. This video covers essential components like leadership commitment, gap analysis, safety objectives, emergency response planning and employee training. Whether you're a small charter operator or interested in aviation safety, this video offers valuable insights into building an effective SMS.

Small operators can leverage SMS implementation to systematize safety oversight, reducing reliance on individual judgment while building organizational safety culture through structured policy and employee engagement.

See NBAA (YouTube) →

Commercial Apr 3, 2026

18 Flights A Day? Delta Air Lines' 10 Most Popular Routes Ranked [List & Map]

Delta plans more flights in April than any other carrier except American. Find out its most-served routes here!

Delta's concentration of frequent service on key routes reflects the carrier's hub-and-spoke strategy to maximize connecting revenue rather than point-to-point competition.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 3, 2026

This Airline Has The World's Most Innovative Business Class Seat In 2026

The seat offers some innovative capabilities.

Direct aisle access from every business class seat reduces service bottlenecks and improves passenger privacy, creating competitive differentiation in premium cabin revenue.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Apr 3, 2026

US and Europe revise GPS interference guidelines as spoofing risks grow

Aviation authorities are warning that instances of GPS interference have spread across a widening range of high-risk airspace,…...

Growing GPS spoofing incidents in critical airspace are forcing regulators to strengthen interference detection standards, elevating operational planning complexity for flight crews and dispatchers.

See AeroTime Hub →

Industry Apr 4, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across airlines, airports, and air traffic systems can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental impacts without requiring wholesale fleet replacement.

See MDPI →

Military Apr 3, 2026

A-10 Thunderbolt II Crashed Near the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Officials Tell NYT

Almost the same time as an F-15E Strike Eagle went down over Iran, the U.S. Air Force lost an A-10 Thunderbolt II attack aircraft near the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. officials told NYT that the jet’s pilot was safely rescued. While search and rescue efforts for at least one of the crew members of an […]...

Two separate U.S. Air Force aircraft losses in the same region within hours suggest heightened operational tempo or threat environment in the Strait of Hormuz area.

See The Aviationist →

Military Apr 3, 2026

The Striking Differences Between The F-35 & F-22

The F-22 Raptor and F-35 Lightning II represent two contrasting philosophies in modern airpower: air superiority versus multirole versatility.

The F-22's specialized air-dominance design contrasts sharply with the F-35's distributed logistics model, creating distinct operational cost and deployment trade-offs for military planners.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Meet NASA’s New Artemis II Science Officers

NASA’s first Artemis II science officers—Kelsey Young, Trevor Graff, and Angela Garcia—are pioneering a new era of mission operations by integrating advanced lunar science directly into Mission Control. Their work will shape how future Artemis crews observe, analyze, and deepen our understanding of...

NASA's new science officer roles embed lunar expertise directly in Mission Control, enabling real-time scientific decision-making that previous missions delegated post-flight, fundamentally changing how crewed lunar operations are conducted.

See NASA →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Italy’s Argotec plans to scale Florida satellite facility to meet rising US demand

Italy’s Argotec has officially opened its first U.S. satellite production facility, cementing a foothold near Kennedy Space Center in Florida to join other foreign space firms pursuing growing demand from American programs.

European aerospace suppliers are establishing domestic US manufacturing to secure contracts in a market increasingly requiring local production capabilities and supply chain resilience.

See SpaceNews →

Military Apr 3, 2026

US F-15E Shot Down Over Iran, One Crew Member Rescued

The fate of the aircraft’s second crew member is unknown.

Loss of a modern fighter-bomber over hostile territory underscores evolving air defense threats and operational risks in contested airspace environments.

See AVweb →

Regulatory Apr 3, 2026

FAA report proposes sweeping rewrite of Part 141 flight training rules

A 471-page industry report released through the FAA’s Part 141 modernization docket proposes a sweeping rewrite of how…...

The comprehensive modernization effort signals the FAA's recognition that 50+ year-old Part 141 regulations require fundamental restructuring to align with contemporary aircraft technology and training methodologies.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Virgil I. “Gus” Grissom

Today marks the 100th anniversary of the birth of Virgil I. “Gus” Grissom, born April 3, 1926, in Mitchell, Indiana. As one of NASA’s first seven astronauts, he became America’s second astronaut to fly in space when he launched aboard the Liberty Bell 7 spacecraft on July 21, 1961, just weeks after...

Grissom's pioneering Mercury-Redstone flight demonstrated that Americans could successfully execute suborbital spaceflight, establishing critical operational protocols for the subsequent Mercury-Atlas orbital missions.

See NASA →

Military Apr 3, 2026

Picture of the Day: April 3, 2026

A C-130J awaits its next assignment on the flightline.

The C-130J's continued operational presence underscores the enduring demand for legacy military cargo platforms despite modernization pressures.

See AVweb →

Electric Apr 3, 2026

ZeroAvia edges closer to cert as FAA issues electric engine conditions

ZeroAvia has this week celebrated a “major step” towards certification of its ZA600 hydrogen-electric powertrain, after the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) published special conditions to certify the system’s electric engine component. The FAA published 33 special conditions for...

FAA's 33 special conditions establish the regulatory framework for hydrogen-electric propulsion certification, enabling ZeroAvia to proceed with system-level validation rather than restarting foundational requirements.

See Runway Girl Network →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

White House again proposes steep NASA budget cuts

For the second consecutive year, the White House is proposing a major budget cut for NASA that would significantly impact the agency’s science programs and the International Space Station.

Sustained NASA funding cuts threaten long-term aerospace innovation and ISS operations, potentially ceding space leadership to international competitors.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Test Pilots and the Road Back to the Moon

For NASA’s first crewed lunar mission since Apollo, aviation experience is one of the clearest threads linking the astronauts aboard Orion.

NASA's Artemis program prioritizes test pilot expertise to navigate unprecedented lunar mission complexities beyond Apollo's original playbook.

See AVweb →

Drones Apr 3, 2026

New DJI Terra update adds HEIF support, boosts reconstruction performance

DJI is continuing to refine its mapping and modeling ecosystem with the latest update to DJI Terra, and version 5.2.0 brings a mix of speed boosts, smarter automation, and improved output quality for enterprise users. more…...

HEIF support and reconstruction performance improvements in DJI Terra enable enterprise users to process larger datasets more efficiently while reducing storage requirements for drone mapping projects.

See DroneDJ →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Hello, World

NASA astronaut and Artemis II Commander Reid Wiseman took this picture of Earth from the Orion spacecraft’s window after completing the translunar injection burn. There are two auroras (top right and bottom left) and zodiacal light (bottom right) is visible as the Earth eclipses the Sun. This and...

Orion's advanced imaging capabilities demonstrate NASA's readiness for deep-space human missions, with natural phenomena visibility confirming spacecraft performance beyond Earth orbit.

See NASA →

Military Apr 3, 2026

US rescues one airman after F-15E shot down over Iran, search continues for second

Unverified images circulating on social media on April 3, 2026, appear to show the wreckage of a US…...

Shoot-down incidents over hostile territory expose critical gaps in combat search-and-rescue coordination and real-time threat assessment capabilities for modern air operations.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Apr 3, 2026

US drone future hinges on policy, investment, coordination

The Commercial Drone Alliance is pushing Washington to move faster, and more strategically, on building a truly domestic drone ecosystem. In a newly released white paper, the industry group calls on the Trump Administration to adopt sweeping reforms aimed at strengthening US manufacturing, creating...

The drone industry's growth depends less on technical capability than on coordinated federal policy and domestic manufacturing investment to compete globally.

See DroneDJ →

General Aviation Apr 3, 2026

Video: Red Bull Wingsuiters Chase Northern Lights Over Alaska

Red Bull Air Force athletes Jeff Provenzano and John D’Vour complete a years-in-the-making night jump under the aurora.

Extreme wingsuit operations in polar night conditions demonstrate advanced human performance capabilities in low-visibility, high-altitude environments with unique safety and navigation challenges.
